steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim states that the survey reveals "strong support for building more HSR lines in the United States." The correct quotation must show a U.S. resident actively supporting the construction of more HSR lines — not past experience, not preference for another transport mode, not satisfaction with the current number.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'I prefer riding buses instead of taking trains because buses travel to more locations.': Expresses preference for buses over trains. This contradicts pro-HSR sentiment and does not illustrate support for building more HSR lines. ✗
  • Choice B — 'I have traveled on high-speed rail lines in Norway but not in Japan.': Reports past travel experience with no opinion expressed about U.S. HSR expansion. Neutral — cannot illustrate "strong support." ✗
  • Choice C — 'I think high-speed rail is wonderful. The United States needs to build more high-speed rail lines.': Directly endorses HSR and explicitly calls for more HSR lines in the United States. Matches the claim precisely. ✓
  • Choice D — 'I believe that the United States has enough high-speed rail lines. It should invest in airports instead.': Opposes further HSR construction. This is the opposite of the support the claim describes. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
